commentaries.author: Athanasius the Apostolic
commentaries.content: Yet the love of God for humanity is such that by grace He becomes Father of those in relation to whom he had previously only been Maker. He becomes their Father when created beings receive ‘into their hearts the Spirit of the Son crying out, ‘Abba, Father’ [Gal 4. , as the apostle says. These are the ones who, by receiving the Word, receive authority from him ‘to become the children of God’ [John 1. Being creatures by nature, they would not become ‘sons’ except by receiving the Spirit of the natural and true Son. So it was in order to bring this about and to make humanity receptive of divinity that ‘The Word became flesh’ (John 1. 14)… Accordingly, the Father calls ‘sons’ those in whom He sees His own Son and He says, ‘I begot’ since ‘begetting’ signifies ‘sons’ while ‘making’ is indicative of the works. Therefore we are not begotten first but made [created], for it is written, ‘Let us make humanity’ (Gen 1. But when we later receive the grace of The Spirit, we are henceforth said to also be begotten.
commentaries.content_en: Yet the love of God for humanity is such that by grace He becomes Father of those in relation to whom he had previously only been Maker. He becomes their Father when created beings receive ‘into their hearts the Spirit of the Son crying out, ‘Abba, Father’ [Gal 4. , as the apostle says. These are the ones who, by receiving the Word, receive authority from him ‘to become the children of God’ [John 1. Being creatures by nature, they would not become ‘sons’ except by receiving the Spirit of the natural and true Son. So it was in order to bring this about and to make humanity receptive of divinity that ‘The Word became flesh’ (John 1. 14)… Accordingly, the Father calls ‘sons’ those in whom He sees His own Son and He says, ‘I begot’ since ‘begetting’ signifies ‘sons’ while ‘making’ is indicative of the works. Therefore we are not begotten first but made [created], for it is written, ‘Let us make humanity’ (Gen 1. But when we later receive the grace of The Spirit, we are henceforth said to also be begotten.
commentaries.content_pt: Contudo, o amor de Deus pela humanidade é tal que, pela graça, Ele se torna Pai daqueles em relação aos quais anteriormente era apenas Criador. Ele se torna seu Pai quando os seres criados recebem ‘em seus corações o Espírito do Filho clamando: ‘Abba, Pai’ [Gal 4.6], como diz o apóstolo. Estes são aqueles que, ao receberem o Verbo, recebem autoridade dele ‘para se tornarem filhos de Deus’ [João 1.12]. Sendo criaturas por natureza, não se tornariam ‘filhos’ exceto ao receberem o Espírito do Filho natural e verdadeiro. Assim, foi para que isso se realizasse e para tornar a humanidade receptiva à divindade que ‘O Verbo se fez carne’ [João 1.14]… Portanto, o Pai chama de ‘filhos’ aqueles em quem Ele vê Seu próprio Filho e diz: ‘Eu gerei’, uma vez que ‘gerar’ significa ‘filhos’, enquanto ‘fazer’ é indicativo das obras. Portanto, não somos gerados primeiro, mas feitos [criados], pois está escrito: ‘Façamos a humanidade’ [Gên 1.26]. Mas quando mais tarde recebemos a graça do Espírito, somos, doravante, também considerados gerados.

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the children of God, even to them that believe on his name: It is not the poor simply, but those that have wished to become poor for righteousness' sake, that He pronounces blessed-those who have despised the honours of this world in order to attain "the good; "likewise also those who, through chastity, have become comely in person and character, and those who are of noble birth, and honourable, having through righteousness attained to adoption, and therefore "have received power to become the sons of God".
commentaries.content_en: But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the children of God, even to them that believe on his name: It is not the poor simply, but those that have wished to become poor for righteousness' sake, that He pronounces blessed-those who have despised the honours of this world in order to attain "the good; "likewise also those who, through chastity, have become comely in person and character, and those who are of noble birth, and honourable, having through righteousness attained to adoption, and therefore "have received power to become the sons of God".
commentaries.content_pt: Mas a todos quantos o receberam, deu-lhes o poder de serem feitos filhos de Deus, a saber, aos que creem no seu nome: Não são os pobres simplesmente, mas aqueles que desejam se tornar pobres por causa da justiça, que Ele pronuncia bem-aventurados – aqueles que desprezaram as honras deste mundo para alcançar ‘o bem’; da mesma forma, também aqueles que, através da castidade, tornaram-se belos em pessoa e caráter, e aqueles que são de nobre nascimento e honrados, que, através da justiça, alcançaram a adoção, e, portanto, ‘receberam poder para se tornarem filhos de Deus’.

commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: A right judgement verily and worthy of God. The firstborn, Israel, is cast out; for he would not abide in ownness |104 with God, nor did lie receive the Son, Who came among His own, he rejected the Bestower of Nobility, he thrust away the Giver of Grace: the Gentiles received Him by faith. Therefore will Israel with reason receive the wages of their folly, they will mourn the loss of good things, they will receive the bitter fruit of their own ill-counsel, bereft of the sonship; and the Gentiles will delight them selves in the good things that are through faith, they shall find the bright rewards of their obedience and shall be planted out in his place. For they shall be cut out of the olive tree which is wild by nature, and be grafted contrary to nature into a good olive tree. And Israel shall hear, Ah sinful nation, a people laden with iniquity, a seed of evildoers, children that are corrupters, they have forsaken the Lord, they have provoked the Holy One of Israel unto anger: but one of Christ's disciples shall say to the Gentiles, But ye are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people, that ye should shew forth the praises of Him Who hath called you out of darkness into His marvellous Light. For since they received the Son through faith, they receive the power to be ranked among the sons of God. For the Son gives what is His alone and specially and of nature to be in their power, setting it forth as common, making this a sort of image of the love for man that is inherent to Him, and of His love for the world. For in none other way could we who bore the image of the earthy escape corruption, unless the beauty of the image of the heavenly were impressed upon us, through our being called to sonship. For being partakers of Him through the Spirit, we were sealed unto likeness with Him and mount up to the primal character of the Image after which the Divine Scripture says we were made. For thus hardly recovering the pristine beauty of our nature, and re-formed unto that Divine Nature, shall we be superior to the ills that have befallen us through the transgression. Therefore we mount up unto dignity above our nature for Christ's sake, and we too shall be sons of God, not like Him in exactitude, but by grace in imitation of Him. For He is Very Son, existing from the Father; we adopted by His Kindness, through grace receiving I have said, Ye are gods and all of you are children of the Most High. For the created and subject nature is called to what is above nature by the mere nod and will of the Father: but the Son and God and Lord will not possess this being God and Son, by the will of God the Father, nor in that He wills it only, but beaming forth of the Very Essence of the Father, He receives to Himself by Nature what is Its own Good. And again He is clearly seen to be Very Son, proved by comparison with ourselves. For since that which is by Nature has another mode of being from that which is by adoption, and that which is in truth from that which is by imitation, and we are called sons of God by adoption and imitation: hence He is Son by Nature and in truth, to Whom we made sons too are compared, gaining the good by grace instead of by natural endowments.
commentaries.content_en: A right judgement verily and worthy of God. The firstborn, Israel, is cast out; for he would not abide in ownness |104 with God, nor did lie receive the Son, Who came among His own, he rejected the Bestower of Nobility, he thrust away the Giver of Grace: the Gentiles received Him by faith. Therefore will Israel with reason receive the wages of their folly, they will mourn the loss of good things, they will receive the bitter fruit of their own ill-counsel, bereft of the sonship; and the Gentiles will delight them selves in the good things that are through faith, they shall find the bright rewards of their obedience and shall be planted out in his place. For they shall be cut out of the olive tree which is wild by nature, and be grafted contrary to nature into a good olive tree. And Israel shall hear, Ah sinful nation, a people laden with iniquity, a seed of evildoers, children that are corrupters, they have forsaken the Lord, they have provoked the Holy One of Israel unto anger: but one of Christ's disciples shall say to the Gentiles, But ye are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people, that ye should shew forth the praises of Him Who hath called you out of darkness into His marvellous Light. For since they received the Son through faith, they receive the power to be ranked among the sons of God. For the Son gives what is His alone and specially and of nature to be in their power, setting it forth as common, making this a sort of image of the love for man that is inherent to Him, and of His love for the world. For in none other way could we who bore the image of the earthy escape corruption, unless the beauty of the image of the heavenly were impressed upon us, through our being called to sonship. For being partakers of Him through the Spirit, we were sealed unto likeness with Him and mount up to the primal character of the Image after which the Divine Scripture says we were made. For thus hardly recovering the pristine beauty of our nature, and re-formed unto that Divine Nature, shall we be superior to the ills that have befallen us through the transgression. Therefore we mount up unto dignity above our nature for Christ's sake, and we too shall be sons of God, not like Him in exactitude, but by grace in imitation of Him. For He is Very Son, existing from the Father; we adopted by His Kindness, through grace receiving I have said, Ye are gods and all of you are children of the Most High. For the created and subject nature is called to what is above nature by the mere nod and will of the Father: but the Son and God and Lord will not possess this being God and Son, by the will of God the Father, nor in that He wills it only, but beaming forth of the Very Essence of the Father, He receives to Himself by Nature what is Its own Good. And again He is clearly seen to be Very Son, proved by comparison with ourselves. For since that which is by Nature has another mode of being from that which is by adoption, and that which is in truth from that which is by imitation, and we are called sons of God by adoption and imitation: hence He is Son by Nature and in truth, to Whom we made sons too are compared, gaining the good by grace instead of by natural endowments.
commentaries.content_pt: Um juízo justo, verdadeiramente digno de Deus. O primogênito, Israel, é expulso; pois ele não quis permanecer em comunhão com Deus, nem recebeu o Filho, que veio entre os seus, rejeitou o Doador de Nobreza, afastou o Doador de Graça: os gentios O receberam pela fé. Portanto, Israel receberá com razão os frutos de sua insensatez, lamentará a perda das coisas boas, receberá o amargo fruto de seu próprio mau conselho, desprovido da filiação; e os gentios se deleitarão nas coisas boas que são através da fé, encontrarão as brilhantes recompensas de sua obediência e serão plantados em seu lugar. Pois eles serão cortados da oliveira que é brava por natureza e enxertados, contra a natureza, em uma boa oliveira. E Israel ouvirá: Ah, nação pecadora, povo carregado de iniquidade, semente de malfeitores, filhos que são corruptores, abandonaram o Senhor, provocaram o Santo de Israel à ira: mas um dos discípulos de Cristo dirá aos gentios: Mas vós sois uma geração eleita, um sacerdócio real, uma nação santa, um povo peculiar, para que possais anunciar as virtudes daquele que vos chamou das trevas para a sua maravilhosa luz. Pois, uma vez que receberam o Filho pela fé, recebem o poder de serem classificados entre os filhos de Deus. Pois o Filho dá o que é Seu e, especialmente por natureza, está em seu poder, apresentando-o como comum, fazendo disso uma espécie de imagem do amor pelo homem que é inerente a Ele e de Seu amor pelo mundo. Pois de nenhuma outra maneira poderíamos nós, que carregamos a imagem do terreno, escapar da corrupção, a menos que a beleza da imagem do celestial fosse impressa em nós, através de nosso chamado à filiação. Pois, sendo participantes d'Ele através do Espírito, fomos selados para a semelhança com Ele e ascendemos ao caráter primal da Imagem segundo a qual as Sagradas Escrituras dizem que fomos feitos. Pois assim, ao recuperar arduamente a beleza primitiva de nossa natureza e ser reformados àquela Natureza Divina, seremos superiores aos males que nos sobrevieram através da transgressão. Portanto, ascendemos à dignidade acima de nossa natureza por amor de Cristo, e também seremos filhos de Deus, não como Ele em exatidão, mas pela graça em imitação d'Ele. Pois Ele é o Verdadeiro Filho, existindo do Pai; nós, adotados por Sua Bondade, recebendo pela graça, como já disse: Vós sois deuses e todos vós sois filhos do Altíssimo. Pois a natureza criada e sujeita é chamada ao que está acima da natureza pelo mero aceno e vontade do Pai: mas o Filho e Deus e Senhor não possuirá este ser Deus e Filho, pela vontade de Deus Pai, nem apenas porque Ele o deseja, mas irradiando a própria Essência do Pai, Ele recebe para Si por Natureza o que é Seu Bem. E novamente, Ele é claramente visto como o Verdadeiro Filho, provado pela comparação conosco. Pois, uma vez que o que é por Natureza tem um modo de ser diferente daquele que é por adoção, e o que é em verdade é diferente do que é por imitação, e nós somos chamados filhos de Deus por adoção e imitação: assim, Ele é Filho por Natureza e em verdade, a Quem nós, que também nos tornamos filhos, somos comparados, ganhando o bem pela graça em vez de por dotes naturais.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the children of God, even to them that believe on his name: He gave to them power to be made the adoptive sons of God, and heirs of the kingdom of heaven. They are made the children of God by believing and by a new spiritual birth in the sacrament of baptism, not of blood; (literally, not of bloods) not by the will, and desires of the flesh, not by the will of men, nor by human generation, as children are first born of their natural parents, but of God, by faith and divine grace.
commentaries.content_en: But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the children of God, even to them that believe on his name: He gave to them power to be made the adoptive sons of God, and heirs of the kingdom of heaven. They are made the children of God by believing and by a new spiritual birth in the sacrament of baptism, not of blood; (literally, not of bloods) not by the will, and desires of the flesh, not by the will of men, nor by human generation, as children are first born of their natural parents, but of God, by faith and divine grace.
commentaries.content_pt: Mas a todos quantos o receberam, deu-lhes o poder de serem feitos filhos de Deus, a saber, aos que creem no seu nome: Ele deu a eles poder para serem feitos os filhos adotivos de Deus e herdeiros do reino dos céus. Eles se tornam filhos de Deus pela fé e por um novo nascimento espiritual no sacramento do batismo, não de sangue; (literalmente, não de sangues) não pela vontade e desejos da carne, não pela vontade dos homens, nem pela geração humana, como as crianças são geradas de seus pais naturais, mas de Deus, pela fé e pela graça divina.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Why then, O blessed one, do you not also tell us the punishment of them who received Him not. You have said that they were 'His own,' and that when 'He came to His own, they received Him not'; but what they shall suffer for this, what punishment they shall undergo, you have not gone on to add. Yet so you would the more have terrified them, and have softened the hardness of their insanity by threatening. Wherefore then have you been silent. And what other punishment, he would say, can be greater than this, that when power is offered them to become sons of God, they do not become so, but willingly deprive themselves of such nobility and honor as this. Although their punishment shall not even stop at this point, that they gain no good, but moreover the unquenchable fire shall receive them, as in going on he has more plainly revealed. But for the present he speaks of the unutterable goods of those who received Him, and sets these words in brief before us, saying, As many as received Him, to them gave He power to become sons of God. Whether bond or free, whether Greeks or barbarians or Scythians, unlearned or learned, female or male, children or old men, in honor or dishonor, rich or poor, rulers or private persons, all, He says, are deemed worthy the same privilege; for faith and the grace of the Spirit, removing the inequality caused by worldly things, has moulded all to one fashion, and stamped them with one impress, the King's. What can equal this lovingkindness. A king, who is framed of the same clay with us, does not deign to enrol among the royal host his fellow-servants, who share the same nature with himself, and in character often are better than he, if they chance to be slaves; but the Only-Begotten Son of God did not disdain to reckon among the company of His children both publicans, sorcerers, and slaves, nay, men of less repute and greater poverty than these, maimed in body, and suffering from ten thousand ills. Such is the power of faith in Him, such the excess of His grace. And as the element of fire, when it meets with ore from the mine, straightway of earth makes it gold, even so and much more Baptism makes those who are washed to be of gold instead of clay; the Spirit at that time falling like fire into our souls, burning up the image of the earthy 1 Corinthians 15:49, and producing the image of the heavenly, fresh coined, bright and glittering, as from the furnace-mould. Why then did he say not that He made them sons of God, but that He gave them power to become sons of God. To show that we need much zeal to keep the image of sonship impressed on us at Baptism, all through without spot or soil ; and at the same time to show that no one shall be able to take this power from us, unless we are the first to deprive ourselves of it. For if among men, those who have received the absolute control of any matters have nearly as much power as those who gave them the charge; much more shall we, who have obtained such honor from God, be, if we do noth ing unworthy of this power, stronger than all; because He who put this honor in our hands is greater and better than all. At the same time too he wishes to show, that not even does grace come upon man irrespectively, but upon those who desire and take pains for it. For it lies in the power of these to become (His) children since if they do not themselves first make the choice, the gift does not come upon them, nor have any effect. Having therefore everywhere excluded compulsion and pointing to (man's) voluntary choice and free power, he has said the same now. For even in these mystical blessings, it is, on the one hand, God's part, to give the grace, on the other, man's to supply faith; and in after time there needs for what remains much earnestness. In order to preserve our purity, it is not sufficient for us merely to have been baptized and to have believed, but we must if we will continually enjoy this brightness, display a life worthy of it. This then is God's work in us. To have been born the mystical Birth, and to have been cleansed from all our former sins, comes from Baptism; but to remain for the future pure, never again after this to admit any stain belongs to our own power and diligence. And this is the reason why he reminds us of the manner of the birth, and by comparison with fleshly pangs shows its excellence, when he says,.
commentaries.content_en: Why then, O blessed one, do you not also tell us the punishment of them who received Him not. You have said that they were 'His own,' and that when 'He came to His own, they received Him not'; but what they shall suffer for this, what punishment they shall undergo, you have not gone on to add. Yet so you would the more have terrified them, and have softened the hardness of their insanity by threatening. Wherefore then have you been silent. And what other punishment, he would say, can be greater than this, that when power is offered them to become sons of God, they do not become so, but willingly deprive themselves of such nobility and honor as this. Whether bond or free, whether Greeks or barbarians or Scythians, unlearned or learned, female or male, children or old men, in honor or dishonor, rich or poor, rulers or private persons, all, He says, are deemed worthy the same privilege; for faith and the grace of the Spirit, removing the inequality caused by worldly things, has moulded all to one fashion, and stamped them with one impress, the King's. For even in these mystical blessings, it is, on the one hand, God's part, to give the grace, on the other, man's to supply faith; and in after time there needs for what remains much earnestness. This then is God's work in us. And this is the reason why he reminds us of the manner of the birth, and by comparison with fleshly pangs shows its excellence, when he says,.
commentaries.content_pt: Por que então, ó abençoado, você não nos diz também a punição daqueles que não O receberam? Você disse que eram 'os Seus', e que quando 'Ele veio para os Seus, não O receberam'; mas o que eles sofrerão por isso, que punição eles enfrentarão, você não acrescentou. No entanto, assim você teria mais aterrorizado a eles, e amolecido a dureza de sua insanidade ameaçando-os. Por que então você permaneceu em silêncio? E que outra punição, diria ele, pode ser maior do que esta, que quando o poder é oferecido a eles para se tornarem filhos de Deus, eles não se tornam, mas voluntariamente se privam de tal nobreza e honra como esta. Embora sua punição não pare nem mesmo neste ponto, que eles não ganham bem algum, mas além disso o fogo inextinguível os receberá, como ao prosseguir ele revelou mais claramente. Mas, por ora, ele fala dos bens inefáveis daqueles que O receberam, e coloca estas palavras em resumo diante de nós, dizendo: Quantos O receberam, a eles deu o poder de se tornarem filhos de Deus. Se escravos ou livres, se gregos ou bárbaros ou citas, não instruídos ou instruídos, mulheres ou homens, crianças ou velhos, em honra ou desonra, ricos ou pobres, governantes ou pessoas comuns, todos, diz ele, são considerados dignos do mesmo privilégio; pois a fé e a graça do Espírito, removendo a desigualdade causada pelas coisas do mundo, moldaram todos a uma só forma, e os marcaram com uma única impressão, a do Rei. O que pode igualar essa bondade. Um rei, que é formado do mesmo barro que nós, não se digna a inscrever entre o hoste real seus semelhantes, que compartilham da mesma natureza que ele, e em caráter muitas vezes são melhores do que ele, se acaso forem escravos; mas o Filho Unigênito de Deus não desprezou contar entre a companhia de Seus filhos tanto publicanos, quanto feiticeiros e escravos, e sim, homens de menor reputação e maior pobreza do que estes, aleijados no corpo, e sofrendo de mil males. Tal é o poder da fé Nele, tal é o excesso de Sua graça. E assim como o elemento do fogo, quando encontra minério da mina, imediatamente transforma a terra em ouro, assim também e muito mais o Batismo faz com que aqueles que são lavados sejam de ouro em vez de barro; o Espírito, naquele momento, caindo como fogo em nossas almas, queimando a imagem do terreno 1 Coríntios 15:49, e produzindo a imagem do celestial, recém cunhada, brilhante e cintilante, como da forma da fornalha. Por que então ele não disse que os fez filhos de Deus, mas que lhes deu poder para se tornarem filhos de Deus? Para mostrar que precisamos de muito zelo para manter a imagem da filiação impressa em nós no Batismo, sempre sem mancha ou sujeira; e ao mesmo tempo para mostrar que ninguém poderá tirar esse poder de nós, a menos que sejamos os primeiros a nos privar dele. Pois se entre os homens, aqueles que receberam o controle absoluto de qualquer assunto têm quase tanto poder quanto aqueles que lhes deram a responsabilidade; muito mais nós, que obtivemos tal honra de Deus, seremos, se não fizermos nada indigno desse poder, mais fortes do que todos; porque Aquele que colocou essa honra em nossas mãos é maior e melhor do que todos. Ao mesmo tempo, ele deseja mostrar que nem mesmo a graça vem sobre o homem de forma indiferente, mas sobre aqueles que desejam e se esforçam por ela. Pois está no poder destes se tornarem (Seus) filhos, já que se eles não fizerem a escolha primeiro, o dom não vem sobre eles, nem tem qualquer efeito. Tendo, portanto, excluído em toda parte a compulsão e apontando para a escolha voluntária e o livre poder do homem, ele disse o mesmo agora. Pois mesmo nessas bênçãos místicas, é, por um lado, parte de Deus, dar a graça, por outro, do homem, fornecer a fé; e em tempos posteriores, é necessário para o que resta muito empenho. Para preservar nossa pureza, não é suficiente apenas termos sido batizados e termos crido, mas devemos, se quisermos desfrutar continuamente dessa luminosidade, exibir uma vida digna dela. Este então é o trabalho de Deus em nós. Ter nascido do Nascimento místico, e ter sido limpo de todos os nossos pecados anteriores, vem do Batismo; mas permanecer para o futuro puro, nunca mais após isso admitir qualquer mancha pertence ao nosso próprio poder e diligência. E esta é a razão pela qual ele nos lembra da maneira do nascimento, e por comparação com as dores carnais mostra sua excelência, quando ele diz.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: The prayer begins with a testimony to God, and with the reward of faith, when we say, "Our Father who art in the heavens; "for (in so saying), we at once pray to God, and commend faith, whose reward this appellation is. It is written, "To them who believed on Him He gave power to be called sons of God."
commentaries.content_en: The prayer begins with a testimony to God, and with the reward of faith, when we say, "Our Father who art in the heavens; "for (in so saying), we at once pray to God, and commend faith, whose reward this appellation is. It is written, "To them who believed on Him He gave power to be called sons of God."
commentaries.content_pt: A oração começa com um testemunho a Deus, e com a recompensa da fé, quando dizemos: "Pai nosso que estás nos céus;" pois (ao dizer isso), nós imediatamente oramos a Deus, e confiamos a fé, cuja recompensa é essa denominação. Está escrito: "Aqueles que creram Nele, Ele deu poder para serem chamados filhos de Deus."

commentaries.author: Theophylact of Ochrid
commentaries.content: Whether slaves or free, children or elders, barbarians or Greeks, as many as received Him, to them gave He power to become sons of God. Who were they who received Him. Those who believed in His name. Those who received the Word and the true Light received and accepted Him through faith. Why did the Evangelist not say that He made them sons of God, but instead that He gave them power to become sons of God. Listen, and I will tell you. To guard the purity of the soul and body, it is not enough to be baptized. We must also exercise much zeal and effort to preserve unspotted the image of adoption imprinted on us in Baptism. Therefore, many who have received the grace of adoption through Baptism were lazy and in the end did not become sons of God. One might add this as well, that many receive Him through faith alone, such as those whom we call the catechumens, but have not yet become sons of God. Yet they indeed have the power to become sons of God if they choose to be baptized and to be counted worthy of this grace, that is, adoption as sons. Furthermore, even if we receive the grace of adoption through Baptism, we will receive the completion and perfection of this grace only in the resurrection, and we hope to receive at that time the fullness of adoption. As Paul says, Even we are waiting for the adoption. 8:23] For all these reasons, then, the Evangelist did not say, "As many as received Him, He made them sons of God," but instead, to them gave He power to become sons of God, that is, to receive this grace in the age to come.
commentaries.content_en: Whether slaves or free, children or elders, barbarians or Greeks, as many as received Him, to them gave He power to become sons of God. Who were they who received Him. Those who believed in His name. Those who received the Word and the true Light received and accepted Him through faith. Why did the Evangelist not say that He made them sons of God, but instead that He gave them power to become sons of God. Listen, and I will tell you. To guard the purity of the soul and body, it is not enough to be baptized. We must also exercise much zeal and effort to preserve unspotted the image of adoption imprinted on us in Baptism. Therefore, many who have received the grace of adoption through Baptism were lazy and in the end did not become sons of God. One might add this as well, that many receive Him through faith alone, such as those whom we call the catechumens, but have not yet become sons of God. Yet they indeed have the power to become sons of God if they choose to be baptized and to be counted worthy of this grace, that is, adoption as sons. Furthermore, even if we receive the grace of adoption through Baptism, we will receive the completion and perfection of this grace only in the resurrection, and we hope to receive at that time the fullness of adoption. As Paul says, Even we are waiting for the adoption. 8:23] For all these reasons, then, the Evangelist did not say, "As many as received Him, He made them sons of God," but instead, to them gave He power to become sons of God, that is, to receive this grace in the age to come.
commentaries.content_pt: Se escravos ou livres, crianças ou idosos, bárbaros ou gregos, quantos O receberam, a eles deu Ele poder para se tornarem filhos de Deus. Quem eram aqueles que O receberam? Aqueles que creram em Seu nome. Aqueles que receberam o Verbum e a verdadeira Luz O receberam e O aceitaram pela fé. Por que o Evangelista não disse que Ele os fez filhos de Deus, mas em vez disso que Ele lhes deu poder para se tornarem filhos de Deus? Escute, e eu lhe direi. Para guardar a pureza da alma e do corpo, não é suficiente ser batizado. Devemos também exercer muito zelo e esforço para preservar imaculada a imagem da adoção impressa em nós no Batismo. Portanto, muitos que receberam a graça da adoção através do Batismo foram preguiçosos e, no final, não se tornaram filhos de Deus. Pode-se acrescentar isso também, que muitos O recebem apenas pela fé, como aqueles que chamamos de catecúmenos, mas ainda não se tornaram filhos de Deus. No entanto, eles de fato têm o poder de se tornarem filhos de Deus se escolherem ser batizados e serem considerados dignos dessa graça, ou seja, a adoção como filhos. Além disso, mesmo que recebamos a graça da adoção através do Batismo, receberemos a conclusão e a perfeição dessa graça apenas na ressurreição, e esperamos receber naquele momento a plenitude da adoção. Como Paulo diz: Até nós estamos aguardando a adoção. [Romanos 8:23] Por todas essas razões, então, o Evangelista não disse: "Quantos O receberam, Ele os fez filhos de Deus," mas em vez disso, a eles deu poder para se tornarem filhos de Deus, ou seja, para receber essa graça na era vindoura.
